revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:هيموروغي]][[en:Himorogi]][[es:Himorogi]][[fa:هیموروگی]][[fi:Himorogi]][[fr:Himorogi]][[id:Himorogi]][[it:Himorogi]][[ja:神籬]][[ko:신리 (신토)]][[pl:Himorogi]][[pt:Himoragi]][[ru:Химороги]][[simple:Himorogi]][[sv:Himorogi]][[zh:神籬]]
{{Short description|Shinto worship space}} {{italics title}} [[File:Tsurugaoka hachimangu himorogi.jpg|thumb|A ''himorogi'' at [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]]]] [[File:Himorogi rokkou.jpg|thumb|''Himorogi'' of [[Ame no Hohi|Amenohohi-no-mikoto]] at [[Rokkosan Country House]].]] {{Nihongo|'''''Himorogi'''''|神籬||{{lit.}} "divine fence"}} in [[Shinto]] terminology are [[:en:Sacred|Sacred]] spaces or {{ill|Altar|en|Altar|qid=Q101687|lt=Altar|1=Altar}}s used to {{ill|Worship|en|Worship|qid=Q2514663|lt=Worship|1=Worship}}.<ref name="sugi">Sugiyama, "Himorogi"</ref> In their simplest form, they are square areas with green bamboo or ''{{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|qid=Q775470|lt=Cleyera japonica|1=Cleyera japonica}}'' at the corners without {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|qid=Q3080012|lt=architecture|1=Shinto architecture}}. These in turn support sacred ropes (''{{ill|Shimenawa|en|Shimenawa|qid=Q1887421|lt=Shimenawa|1=Shimenawa}}'') decorated with streamers called ''{{ill|Shide (Shinto)|en|Shide (Shinto)|qid=Q2657982|lt=shide|1=Shide (Shinto)}}''.<ref name="sugi"/> A branch of ''sakaki'' or some other evergreen at the center acts as a ''{{ill|Yorishiro|en|Yorishiro|qid=Q333798|lt=Yorishiro|1=Yorishiro}}'', a physical representation of the presence of the ''[[kami]]'', a being which is in itself incorporeal.<ref name="sugi"/><ref>Kōjien, 神籬</ref>
[[File:Jichinsai-himorogi.jpg|thumb|left|A ''himorogi'' built for a ''jichinsai'']]During the Aoi Festival in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}} the ''himorogi'' is a square space surrounded by green branches with an {{ill|Evergreen|en|Evergreen|qid=Q190489|lt=Evergreen|1=Evergreen}} tree at the center as a ''yorishiro''.<ref name="sugi"/> A more elaborate ''himorogi'' can also be made with a straw mat on the ground with on it a ceremonial 8-legged stand called an ''{{nihongo|hassoku-[[An (Shinto)|an]]|八足案|extra=eight leg an}}'' decorated with ''shimenawa'' and sacred emblems.<ref name="sugi"/>
The etymology of the word is unclear, but it appears already in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and in the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|qid=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū|1=Man'yōshū}}''.<ref name="sugi"/> The term "himorogi" refers equally to the focal point "tree" and to the sacred space, both of which are deemed to be purified or "unpolluted".<ref>Bocking, Brian. (1997). ''A Popular Dictionary of Shinto.'' p. 41.</ref>
''Himorogi'' in Japan are most commonly seen at {{ill|Construction|en|Construction|qid=Q3875186|lt=Construction|1=Construction}} sites, where after use they stand for a while before actual work begins. They are built for a [[kannushi|Shinto priest]], who comes to {{ill|Blessing|en|Blessing|qid=Q626510|lt=bless|1=Blessing}} the site during a ground-breaking ceremony called {{nihongo|''[[jichinsai]]''|地鎮祭}} {{Wiktionary|神籬|1=Himorogi}}
== See also == * [[Ko-Shintō|Koshintō]] * [[Shintai]] * The [[Glossary of Shinto]] for an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Shinto, Shinto art, and Shinto shrine architecture. *{{ill|Hiis (sacred site)|en|Hiis (sacred site)|qid=Q19847629|lt=Hiis (sacred site)|1=Hiis (sacred site)}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References== * Bocking, Brian. (1997). ''A Popular Dictionary of Shinto.'' Lincolnwood, Illinois: NTC Publishing. {{ISBN|9780844204253}}; [https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/36977290 OCLC 36977290] *Iwanami {{ill|Kōjien|en|Kōjien|qid=Q1192611|lt=Kōjien|1=Kōjien}} (広辞苑) Japanese dictionary, 6th Edition (2008), DVD version * {{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=247 |title=Himorogi |last=Sugiyama |first=Shigetsugu |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2008-07-18 }} {{Commons Category|Himorogi}} <!--this was an inappropriate and erroneous transclusion meant to be a bottom bar thing I think but not including--> {{Shinto-stub}}
[[en:Himorogi]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ar:هيموروغي]] {{Commons category|Himorogi}} [[es:Himorogi]] [[fa:هیموروگی]] [[fi:Himorogi]] [[fr:Himorogi]] [[id:Himorogi]] [[it:Himorogi]] [[ja:神籬]] [[ko:신리 (신토)]] [[pl:Himorogi]] [[pt:Himoragi]] [[ru:Химороги]] [[simple:Himorogi]] [[sv:Himorogi]] [[zh:神籬]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q60587}} [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Shinto religious objects]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
